rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JACQUELINE WARD 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/15/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of ALL vested or contingent beneficiaries, not just the contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] There was no order dated 4/8/19 in either this case or the related conservatorship case, as alleged in paragraph 2 of the "Supplemental Declaration" filed on 9/27/21. Probate Code Section 10564 applies to decedents' estates and personal representatives having full IAEA authority, not to a protective proceeding. Section 4.2 of the trust appears to authorize delayed distribution. In any event, the prudent person rule is not the only rule that applies to trusts under Court supervision. [LRSF 14.48 B.5.] The order dated 1/23/13 authorized a $300 monthly allowance to the current beneficiary. The cash allowance was increased to $500 per month by the order dated 11/7/19. An order dated 3/29/13 (not 3/19/13) authorized the trustee to pay Shaunice Suazo up to $200/month to provide transportation for the current beneficiary, subject to approval at the time of the [next] accounting, and Shaunice Suazo was to keep and provide records. Does she still provide transportation for the trust beneficiary? The petition caption actually requests that bond be reduced, but - as with most of the prior accountings - bond needed to be increased but was not done promptly upon filing the accounting petition, resulting in further delays. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/6/21. After these issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required in connection with the fee request for the prior attorney. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DARLENE J FULLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/15/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The order dated 5/9/19 approved the $2,633.15 in costs that appear to have been paid already on 8/31/17, as reflected in the second accounting. Still not all disbursements show who the original payee was (e.g., funeral services paid to the conservator). The attorney costs ("filing fees") attached to the supplemental account total $1,031.50, but Schedule C shows $1,000 without any explanation. The supplemental accounting shows just cash assets on hand at the end of the accounting period (as of 1/20/21, and on a schedule that precedes, rather than follows, the detailed schedules supporting the Summary of Account) but no schedule showing non-cash assets. (The Letters were issued in the related estate case on 1/20/21. That should not have been "corrected" to 1/21/21 in this amended petition.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/6/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WOON KUEN LOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the proposed distribution shows a non-pro rata distribution of certain funds not equally divisible by 6, and the waivers of accounting (which should have been of the administrator, not the executor) did not also include an agreement to the non-pro rata distribution of those funds. [LRSF 14.36 I.6.a.ii.] Also, the attorney costs include $30 for E-filing fees for this final report, when E-filing costs are usually just $5 per document.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MUI TAI VONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/29/2021. The following issues remain unaddressed since the previous tentative rulings: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet and cannot be e-filed. 4) The proof of publication shows the first publication was made less than 15 days before the first hearing date. Notice must be republished with the continued hearing date. 5) Item 2d of the petition must be completed (at least one box must be checked). 6) Re item 3f(2): The will is not self-proving as it was not witnessed. Probate Code section 6110(c)(2) must be addressed. 7) Item 3h(3) or (4) must be checked. 8) Re item 8: Is Jimmy Cam's street number accurate? The proof of service shows 919 instead of 1919. 9) If the attorney represented the decedent, is the attorney ethically allowed to represent the petitioners asserting adverse interests as creditors against the decedent's estate? 10) The objection filed on 08/20/2021 was not verified. Also, no Exhibits were attached. No courtesy copy of the objection has been received. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GRACE MAUBERRET 2009 IRREVOCABLE SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Termination Of Irrevocable Special Needs Trust; Payment Of Liens; Distribution Of Trust Assets To Beneficiary; And Discharge Of Co-Truste
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/18/2022 at request of counsel. No further continuances will be granted without a hearing. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.
